description_long: This object is a cluster of ginger rhizomes, characterized by their irregular, knobby, and bulbous shapes. The surface is rough and covered in a thin, light brown skin with some areas showing slightly darker or orange-brown patches. The ginger pieces are interconnected, forming a compact group with visible nodes and small protrusions typical of fresh ginger. The texture appears fibrous and dense, with some subtle creases and indentations along the surface. The overall appearance suggests a fresh, unpeeled ginger root, commonly found in markets or kitchens. The coloration and organic form make it easily recognizable as a natural, edible vegetable.
description: A cluster of knobby, light brown ginger rhizomes with a rough, fibrous surface and irregular, bulbous shapes.
description_view_0: Multiple ginger rhizomes are grouped together, showing their knobby, interconnected forms and rough, light brown skin.
description_view_1: Two main ginger pieces are visible, highlighting their elongated, cylindrical shapes and subtle surface creases.
description_view_2: The ginger cluster is seen from the side, emphasizing the length and curvature of the rhizomes with visible nodes.
description_view_3: A single ginger piece is most prominent, with its bulbous end and rough, textured surface clearly visible.
